full_response:
Let the spacetime dimension be n.  
By definition the inverse metric satisfies

g^{ac}\,g_{cb}=δ^{a}{}_{b} .                             (1)

Contract (1) on the free indices a and b:

g^{ac} g_{ca}=δ^{a}{}_{a}=n .                            (2)

Now simply relabel the dummy indices c↔b in the left–hand side of (2); dummy indices can be renamed at will, so

g^{ac} g_{ca}=g^{ba} g_{ab}=g_{ab} g^{ba}.               (3)

Hence the scalar is just the dimension of spacetime:

g_{ab} g^{ba}=n.

Therefore

• In 2-dimensional spacetime (n = 2): g_{ab} g^{ba} = 2  
• In 3-dimensional spacetime (n = 3): g_{ab} g^{ba} = 3  
• In 4-dimensional spacetime (n = 4): g_{ab} g^{ba} = 4

\boxed{g_{ab}g^{ba}=2,\;3,\;4\;\text{in 2D, 3D, and 4D respectively}}

Confidence: 98%
